decision: Live channel: use documented ≤2s polling fallback over REST, not the Watchtower /ws bus
rationale: Watchtower's only live channel is an unauthenticated ingest/echo WebSocket bus (/ws, port 8080) not designed for scoped consumer subscription; bridging a durable upstream socket through a Next.js App Router route handler across both host and container deploy modes is fragile and disproportionate. Polling the existing REST proxy pattern (new /api/watchtower/attention aggregation) at 2s meets the ≤2s AC.
alternatives: SSE passthrough bridging watchtower /ws (rejected: fragile across deploy modes, unauth bus); direct browser WS to watchtower (rejected: cross-origin, unauth, unscoped)

decision: Status-derivation is a pure function in lib/attention/status.ts consuming an abstract event model, decoupled from Watchtower wire format and React
rationale: AC6 requires unit-tested pure logic. Adapter (lib/attention/adapter.ts) maps REST sessions/tools into abstract events; deriveStatus handles out-of-order, duplicate, malformed events and recency decay of working->idle.

decision: AC deviation: 🟡 waiting-for-input not sourced live; ✅ done shown only transiently
rationale: Watchtower persists Notification events but exposes no REST endpoint to read them (only sessions/prompts/tools). deriveStatus fully supports+tests waiting, but the REST adapter cannot source it until a /api/sessions/{id}/events endpoint exists. Board scopes to ACTIVE sessions, so done appears only for a session ending while displayed.
